key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that still exhibit martensitic transformation and shape-memory behavior, overcoming a major fabrication barrier for functional metal coatings.
engineering_relevance: This enables durable NiTi surface coatings on steel components for wear, corrosion, friction, and thermal-actuation functions where conventional ceramic coatings are less suitable.
method: Evaluated composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and functional thermomechanical response
evidence_signal: The coatings were assessed with microscopy and phase/functional tests, and showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation and shape-memory response in 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and require annealing; their limited tensile strength may constrain structural use.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
